Introductions: DESCRIPTION: Fuse Add Scalar Into Conv Operation
This transformation pattern searches for a Conv operation followed by an Add
operation where the Add node adds a scalar value to the Conv output. When
found, this pattern fuses these two operations by:
1. Modifying the Conv node's bias parameter by adding the scalar value
3. Directing the Conv output directly to the Add node's output
This optimization reduces model complexity and improves inference performance
by reducing the number of operations and memory allocations.
- Add node must have its second input as an initializer (constant)
- Add node's second input must be a scalar value
- Conv node must directly feed into the Add node
RESULT: Single Conv operation with fused bias instead of Conv + Add

inputs: ((IsInitializer({add_node.input[1]}) == True) and (IsScalar({add_node.input[1]}) == True)) or ((IsInitializer({add_node.input[0]}) == True) and (IsScalar({add_node.input[0]}) == True))

initializers.name: rs_axes
initializers.data: numpy.add({node.input[2]}, {add_node.input[1]}) or numpy.add({node.input[2]}, {add_node.input[0]}) or numpy.tile(numpy.array({add_node.input[0]}), GetShape(({node.output[0]})[-3])) or numpy.tile(numpy.array({add_node.input[1]}), GetShape(({node.output[0]})[-3]))
